body:
# Get help

## Ask a question

I have been using base-ui with tiptap/prosemirror library. It tracks the content editable area for mutations and sync or recalculate it's state if DOM changes.

If I am using nodeView or reactNodeView to render anything inside this area then I need to be very careful of where I place my Popover/Menu trigger or portal as they will add data-base-ui-inert to all the sibling which will trigger the resync with prosemirror

Having these components change the DOM unexpectedly makes it volatile to use as it's a side-effect which may result in unexpected behaviours especially when DOM is being observed by me or any third party library.

There must be a better way to do this, according to my understanding this approach is used to -
1. Trap focus inside Popup and avoid keyboard navigation to select elements outside of Popup area
2. Enable click on an element outside popover area and not register it as outside click if it doesn't have the inert attribute in it's ancestor

Outside click have been solved by hundreds of component libraries, can't those be considered, just want to understand the approach and if there's any fundamental issue that I am missing

Thank you for support!

comments.body:
> Enable click on an element outside popover area and not register it as outside click if it doesn't have the inert attribute in it's ancestor

Yes, this is the main reason - it's to support 3rd party extension elements so clicking on them won't close the popover as it won't be considered an "outside" click.

In the case of `Dialog`, it also adds `aria-hidden="true"` to every outside element, which is a similar mutation to `data-base-ui-inert`. Does that one not cause problems? Dialog also requires an explicit click on the backdrop if it's `modal`, which Popover doesn't require

But it can easily be opt-in instead of forcing it by default, like instead of checking data-base-ui-inert in ancestors to decide whether to close Popup or not we can have data-base-ui-popup or something like that and not close popup when it's present in ancestor, this way user will be making conscious decision of opting in not to close popup when clicked inside an area outside of popup 

Having data-base-ui-inert on backdrop is not an issue as it's an element added by Popover itself

Problem arises only when popover mutates elements in DOM unrelated to Popover/Menu, and yes attributes like aria-hidden="true" also causes problem. Mutation on root elements like body for scroll lock does not cause problem as they are rarely observed but since it mutates almost all sibling elements it cause issues

Appreciate the response, Thanks!

`data-base-ui-inert` marks every current outside element at mount time, so popups from extensions injected after (the typical case) don't get marked and so don't close when pressed. The alternative is storing every "outside" element in memory and comparing by reference instead of the attr which is more expensive

With your suggestion of `data-base-ui-popup`, the extension element isn't controlled by you, so it can't be marked by your code (easily) to block outside clicks on it

I understand the use-case, will keep it in mind. Will share details if I hit a wall while working with libraries that often depends on DOM mutations to work like prosemirror's content editable area

Thanks again!

One solution is a prop of elements that you can pass (e.g. the prosemirror container) to prevent `data-base-ui-inert` from getting set on anything inside of it, or the attrs to instead mark the highest possible ancestor

That would solve my immediate issue, but I can also solve it by placing both popover trigger and popup outside of prosemirror container, would be hassle but it'll achieve the same output

Ideally I would expect it to not mutate unrelated DOM at all, but there must have been a reason to use this approach by base-ui team, at least that's what I expect

I think adding it in docs to let consumer know about this side-effect would be better for now

@ziyadshaikh013 try out the version in #3955 

```
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui/react@3955
```

It mutates the highest level node (app element root/before `<body>`), which avoids issues with editors that watch for node mutations while supporting 3rd party extension elements. It's assumed they append their popups to `<body>` outside of the app tree

The issue I encounter is that base-ui **Dialog** taks a long time to appear under the following conditions: 1. The ProseMirror editor contains a large number of nodes (several thousands or more). 2. At least one of those nodes renders an element with an `aria-live` attribute somewhere in its DOM subtree. ``` Like this:

... ``` > [!NOTE] > A real use case is embedding CodeMirror as an editable code block in ProseMirror. CodeMirror's DOM tree contains an element with `aria-live="polite"` attribute. ([source](https://github.com/codemirror/view/blob/038f0ae53b69f6759d22393dbe984b969d38ba82/src/editorview.ts#L188)) ## Reproduction https://stackblitz.com/edit/base-ui-dialog-perf-issue?file=src%2FApp.tsx The reproduction project demonstrates using **Dialog** to implement a global quick search popup that opens with the Ctrl/Cmd + P keyboard shortcut. When the dialog opens, I observed the following behavior: * base-ui adds `data-base-ui-inert` and `aria-hidden="true"` to all elements inside the ProseMirror `

` container. * This triggers the [`MutationObserver`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/MutationObserver) callback in [`prosemirror-view`](https://github.com/ProseMirror/prosemirror-view/blob/9df3a26f4518f6dfd7fb00db775f93916b2d8110/src/domobserver.ts#L53). * As a result, ProseMirror re-syncs the DOM with its internal document model. * ProseMirror removes those attributes because they don't exist in the document model. The re-sync process takes roughly **150× longer** than the time base-ui spends setting those DOM attributes, which causes the noticeable delay when opening the dialog. > [!NOTE] > ProseMirror re-syncing 3000 paragraphs: > > > > base-ui setting attributes on 3000 paragraphs: > >

@dragonman225 this is a tricky one

`aria-live` elements are excluded from `aria-hidden="true"`  in order for content changes inside that node to be announced/visible to assistive tech while the dialog is open. So https://github.com/mui/base-ui/pull/3955 doesn't fix the perf issue either (from what I can see) in your specific case.

In order for that node to be "excluded", all the siblings (instead of the parent) are marked, which causes that perf issue. Ideally, ProseMirror would just be more lenient with attributes it doesn't recognize (not sure why it's so strict).

Couple of solutions:


1. An escape hatch prop to force mark everything inside the editor as hidden and ignore the `aria-live` element while the dialog is open.
2. Wrap all the siblings in a new `<div>` so `markOthers` only has to mutate one single element and ProseMirror won't be so slow to update all the siblings

Thanks for the explanation and the suggestions!

Along with the solutions you suggested, I found two simpler approaches that solve the performance issue:

1. Set ProseMirror to read-only before opening the dialog to stop it from reacting to DOM mutations.
2. Mark ProseMirror's root [`contenteditable`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Global_attributes/contenteditable) element with `aria-live="off"`, so base-ui would not modify the DOM subtree. From my research, it seems `aria-live="off"` acts the same as not setting `aria-live` at all from the user's view. However, I’m curious if there are any accessibility concerns with this approach.

This fix will be available in the next npm release of Base UI.

In the meantime, you can try it out on our Canary release channel:

```sh
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui/react@3955
```

labels.name: support: question
labels.description: Community support but can be turned into an improvement.

labels.name: component: popover
labels.description: Changes related to the popover component.

title: Adding data-base-ui-inert to DOM elements when floating elements open makes component usage restrictive and unpredictable
